label: Stacks
description: A new chapter introduced in the presentation.

label: Presentation
description: Introduces the concepts of stacks.

label: Outline of Presentation
description: The outline of this presentation.

label: Definition of Stack
description: What a stack is all about.

label: Real Life Examples of Stacks
description: Some real life examples of stacks.

label: Stack as an ADT
description: What is a stack as an ADT (Abstract Data Type).

label: Abstract Data Type (ADT)
description: User's point of view on stack operations without implementation details.

label: Stack Operations
description: Primary and secondary operations supported in case of stack.

label: Primary Operations
description: A category of stack operations.

label: Secondary Operations
description: Another category of stack operations.

label: Linear Data Structure
description: A stack is a linear data structure.

label: Insertions
description: Allowed only at the end called the top of the stack in a stack.

label: Deletions
description: Allowed only at the end called the top of the stack in a stack.

label: Top of the Stack
description: The end of the stack where insertions and deletions are allowed.

label: Array
description: A type of linear data structure, like stack or linked list.

label: Linked List
description: A type of linear data structure, like stack or array.

label: Stack of Books
description: A real life example of stacks.

label: Stack of Coins
description: A real life example of stacks.

label: Access Object
description: To retrieve an object, usually the top most one in a stack example.

label: Add Object
description: To place an object, usually at the top most place in a stack example.

label: Glass Jar Analogy
description: A way to imagine a stack where objects are placed inside, making it easier to visualize restrictions.

label: Closed at Bottom
description: A characteristic of the glass jar in the analogy, implying no access from below.

label: Opened at Top
description: A characteristic of the glass jar in the analogy, implying access from above.

label: One Way to Insert/Delete
description: The singular method for modifying objects in a stack (from the top).

label: User Point of View
description: The perspective from which stack operations are considered when defining a stack as an ADT.

label: Implementation Details
description: How stack operations are performed, which is not of interest when defining a stack as an ADT from a user's perspective.

label: Push Operation
description: A primary stack operation which actually allows us to insert data onto stack.

label: Pop Operation
description: A primary stack operation which simply deletes the last inserted element from the stack.

label: Top Operation
description: A secondary stack operation which simply returns the last inserted element without removing it.

label: Size Operation
description: A secondary stack operation which simply returns the size or the number of elements in the stack.

label: IsEmpty Function
description: A secondary stack operation which simply returns true if the stack is empty, else it returns false.

label: IsFull Function
description: A secondary stack operation which simply returns true if the stack is full, else it returns false.

label: List of Commands
description: A sequence of stack commands to be executed.

label: Push(1)
description: A command to push the element '1' inside the stack.

label: Push(2)
description: A command to push the element '2' inside the stack.

label: Push(3)
description: A command to push the element '3' onto the stack.

label: Pop()
description: A command to delete the top most element from the stack.

label: IsFull()
description: A command to check if the stack is full.
